
title: "STAT 380 Final Project"
author: "Samarth Patel"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(randomForest) 
library(e1071)         
library(pROC)         

set.seed(123)
```

```{r}
# setup and data loading

# load raw data

features_raw <- read.csv("C:/Users/samar/Downloads/STAT 380-2/Project/features.csv",
stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
stores_raw   <- read.csv("C:/Users/samar/Downloads/STAT 380-2/Project/stores.csv",
stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
sales_raw    <- read.csv("C:/Users/samar/Downloads/STAT 380-2/Project/train.csv",
stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# type fixes

# convert Date to Date

features_raw$Date <- as.Date(features_raw$Date)
sales_raw$Date    <- as.Date(sales_raw$Date)

# convert IsHoliday to logical

features_raw$IsHoliday <- as.logical(features_raw$IsHoliday)
sales_raw$IsHoliday    <- as.logical(sales_raw$IsHoliday)

# store Type as factor

stores_raw$Type <- as.factor(stores_raw$Type)

# merge into a single dataset

walmart <- sales_raw %>%
left_join(stores_raw,   by = "Store") %>%
left_join(features_raw, by = c("Store", "Date"))

# combine IsHoliday.x / IsHoliday.y into single IsHoliday

walmart <- walmart %>%
mutate(IsHoliday = IsHoliday.x) %>%  # they should match
select(-IsHoliday.x, -IsHoliday.y)

glimpse(walmart)

# handle missing values

# numeric vs categorical/logical

num_cols <- walmart %>%
dplyr::select(where(is.numeric)) %>%
colnames()

cat_cols <- walmart %>%
dplyr::select(where(function(x) is.character(x) || is.factor(x) || is.logical(x))) %>%
colnames()

# median impute numeric

for (v in num_cols) {
med <- median(walmart[[v]], na.rm = TRUE)
walmart[[v]][is.na(walmart[[v]])] <- med
}

# mode helper for categorical / logical

get_mode <- function(x) {
ux <- na.omit(x)
ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

# mode impute categorical / logical

for (v in cat_cols) {
mode_val <- get_mode(walmart[[v]])
walmart[[v]][is.na(walmart[[v]])] <- mode_val
}

# make sure types are correct

if ("Type" %in% names(walmart)) {
walmart$Type <- as.factor(walmart$Type)
}
if ("IsHoliday" %in% names(walmart)) {
walmart$IsHoliday <- as.logical(walmart$IsHoliday)
}
```


```{r}
# feature engineering

walmart <- walmart %>%
mutate(
Year  = year(Date),
Month = month(Date),          # numeric month 1-12
Week  = isoweek(Date),
log_weekly_sales = ifelse(Weekly_Sales > 0, log(Weekly_Sales), NA_real_)
)

# drop rows where log_weekly_sales is NA (should be very few)

walmart <- walmart %>%
filter(!is.na(log_weekly_sales))

summary(walmart$log_weekly_sales)
```


```{r}
# RQ1 – monthly pattern in sales

# how do average weekly sales vary across different months of the year?

monthly_sales <- walmart %>%
group_by(Month) %>%
summarise(
avg_weekly_sales    = mean(Weekly_Sales),
median_weekly_sales = median(Weekly_Sales),
n = n(),
.groups = "drop"
)

monthly_sales

ggplot(monthly_sales, aes(x = factor(Month), y = avg_weekly_sales)) +
geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
labs(
title = "Average Weekly Sales by Month",
x = "Month (1 = Jan, 12 = Dec)",
y = "Average Weekly Sales"
) +
theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# RQ2 – holiday vs non-holiday weeks

# are average weekly sales higher in holiday weeks than in non-holiday weeks?

holiday_summary <- walmart %>%
group_by(IsHoliday) %>%
summarise(
avg_weekly_sales    = mean(Weekly_Sales),
median_weekly_sales = median(Weekly_Sales),
n = n(),
.groups = "drop"
)

holiday_summary

ggplot(holiday_summary, aes(x = IsHoliday, y = avg_weekly_sales)) +
geom_col(fill ="red") +
labs(
title = "Average Weekly Sales: Holiday vs Non-holiday Weeks",
x = "Is Holiday Week?",
y = "Average Weekly Sales"
) +
theme_minimal()

# t-test for difference in means (holiday vs non-holiday)

t_test_result <- t.test(Weekly_Sales ~ IsHoliday, data = walmart)
t_test_result
```


```{r}
# RQ3 – unemployment vs weekly sales

# is there a relationship between the local unemployment rate and weekly sales?

# correlation between Weekly_Sales and Unemployment
cor_unemp_sales <- cor(walmart$Weekly_Sales, walmart$Unemployment)
cor_unemp_sales

# Task 2 – polynomial regression model for sales 
lm_sales <- lm(
  Weekly_Sales ~ poly(Unemployment, 2, raw = TRUE) + IsHoliday + Month + Store + Size,
  data = walmart
)

summary(lm_sales)

# scatterplot with polynomial regression curve (sample for speed)
set.seed(123)
walmart_plot_sample <- walmart %>%
  slice_sample(n = 5000)

ggplot(walmart_plot_sample, aes(x = Unemployment, y = Weekly_Sales)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 2, raw = TRUE),
    se = TRUE,
    color = "red"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Weekly Sales vs Unemployment Rate (Polynomial Fit, Degree 2)",
    x = "Unemployment Rate",
    y = "Weekly Sales"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```


```{r}
# Task 3 – classification

# RQ: which model best predicts whether a store-week has High or Low sales?

# create binary outcome: High vs Low sales

sales_median <- median(walmart$Weekly_Sales, na.rm = TRUE)

walmart <- walmart %>%
mutate(
High_Sales = if_else(Weekly_Sales >= sales_median, "High", "Low"),
High_Sales = factor(High_Sales, levels = c("Low", "High"))
)

table(walmart$High_Sales)

# build smaller modeling sample for classification (about 2,000 rows total)

set.seed(123)
walmart_clf <- walmart %>%
filter(!is.na(High_Sales)) %>%
group_by(High_Sales) %>%
slice_sample(n = 1000) %>%   # 1000 from each class
ungroup()

nrow(walmart_clf)

glimpse(walmart_clf)

# train / test split for classification

set.seed(123)
n_clf <- nrow(walmart_clf)
train_idx_clf <- sample(1:n_clf, size = floor(0.7 * n_clf))

train_data <- walmart_clf[train_idx_clf, ]
test_data  <- walmart_clf[-train_idx_clf, ]

table(train_data$High_Sales)
table(test_data$High_Sales)

# helper for accuracy

accuracy <- function(pred, truth) {
mean(pred == truth)
}

# predictor set for classification (keep it simple)

predictors_clf <- c(
"Store", "Size",
"Temperature", "Fuel_Price", "CPI", "Unemployment",
"IsHoliday", "Year", "Week"
)

predictors_clf <- predictors_clf[predictors_clf %in% names(train_data)]
predictors_clf

formula_clf <- as.formula(
paste("High_Sales ~", paste(predictors_clf, collapse = " + "))
)
```


```{r}
# logistic regression 

logit_model <- glm(formula_clf, data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(logit_model)

logit_prob <- predict(logit_model, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
logit_pred <- ifelse(logit_prob >= 0.5, "High", "Low") |>
factor(levels = c("Low", "High"))

table(Predicted = logit_pred, Actual = test_data$High_Sales)

roc_logit <- roc(test_data$High_Sales, logit_prob, levels = c("Low", "High"))
auc_logit <- auc(roc_logit)
auc_logit

acc_logit <- accuracy(logit_pred, test_data$High_Sales)
acc_logit
```


```{r}
# random forest 

set.seed(123)
rf_model <- randomForest(
formula_clf,
data = train_data,
ntree = 50,   # small number of trees
mtry = 3,
importance = TRUE
)

rf_prob <- predict(rf_model, newdata = test_data, type = "prob")
rf_pred <- ifelse(rf_prob[, "High"] >= 0.5, "High", "Low") |>
factor(levels = c("Low", "High"))

table(Predicted = rf_pred, Actual = test_data$High_Sales)

roc_rf <- roc(test_data$High_Sales, rf_prob[, "High"], levels = c("Low", "High"))
auc_rf <- auc(roc_rf)
auc_rf

acc_rf <- accuracy(rf_pred, test_data$High_Sales)
acc_rf

# variable importance plot (optional)

varImpPlot(rf_model)
```


```{r}
# naive Bayes

nb_model <- naiveBayes(formula_clf, data = train_data)

nb_prob <- predict(nb_model, newdata = test_data, type = "raw")[, "High"]
nb_pred <- ifelse(nb_prob >= 0.5, "High", "Low") |>
factor(levels = c("Low", "High"))

table(Predicted = nb_pred, Actual = test_data$High_Sales)

roc_nb <- roc(test_data$High_Sales, nb_prob, levels = c("Low", "High"))
auc_nb <- auc(roc_nb)
auc_nb

acc_nb <- accuracy(nb_pred, test_data$High_Sales)
acc_nb
```


```{r}
# model comparison summary for Task 3

model_summary <- tibble(
Model    = c("Logistic Regression", "Random Forest", "Naive Bayes"),
AUC      = c(as.numeric(auc_logit),
as.numeric(auc_rf),
as.numeric(auc_nb)),
Accuracy = c(acc_logit, acc_rf, acc_nb)
)

model_summary
```


```{r}
# simple printed summary for the write-up

cat("RQ1 – Monthly pattern in sales\n")
print(monthly_sales)
cat("\n")

cat("RQ2 – Holiday vs non-holiday weeks\n")
print(holiday_summary)
cat("\n")
cat("t-test p-value (Weekly_Sales ~ IsHoliday):", t_test_result$p.value, "\n\n")

cat("RQ3 – Unemployment vs weekly sales\n")
cat("Correlation between Weekly_Sales and Unemployment:", cor_unemp_sales, "\n")
cat("See regression summary for lm_sales for the effect of Unemployment and controls.\n\n")

cat("Task 3 – Classification: High vs Low sales\n")
print(model_summary)
cat("\n")
cat("Best model can be chosen by highest AUC (and/or accuracy).\n")

```
